Event Name/Title

How To Be Krishna Conscious

Event Date

21 Jul 1989

Event Location

India, Mumbai

Event Type

Other

Transcriber

Sanatan Rsi Das

Completed

26 Jun 2021

Tags – Places

e.g. Govardhan, Radha Kund, Shyama Kund

Tags – Pastimes

Tags – Themes

e.g. Surrender, Patience, Service, Mercy, Heart

Q&A Transcription

Problems in spiritual life so I realize that … broke off it was very good for me spiritually, I realize that, but my mind why does my mind is still lamenting? In spite of knowing I... understand …

That is why Krishna in third chapter of Gita tell us you must conquer lower side with higher side. Lower self is the desires of senses as well as the desires of the mind, among our emotional attachments are based on our minds sense gratification. The higher self is the intelligence given by God. The difference between a human being and animal is that man is called a rational animal and if we have an intelligence by which we can discriminate and that when that discrimination is based on the Word of God, that is real intelligence, that is a higher self. The Higher self is the soul, the lower self is the body and mind, the sense and the mind. So when our activities are judged very discreetly on the basis of that higher intelligence given by God that is Krishna Consciousness. So sometimes senses and mind are very attached to certain people and certain things of this world that are not favorable for our again to our spiritual life. Those things must be rejected. Rupa Goswami describes to make advancement in Krishna consciousness is very simple you accept those things favorable for spiritual life and reject those things unfavorable for spiritual life. So we have to understand what is good for me and what is not good for me on that basis. Now the fact that we lament over something that we are conditioned to enjoy when we give it up is natural, Krishna says in Gita tatra tisthami Narada, I am sorry ah! he says that the non-permanent appearance of happiness and distress and its disappearance in due course unlike the appearance and disappearance of the winter and summer seasons, one must learn to tolerate them without being disturbed so these attachments that we have in this world we have to learn to tolerate the happiness and the lamentation, the pleasure and the pain, the honor and the dishonor. So considered it is nothing bad, it is a natural thing. With our intelligence we have to take proper action and we have to tolerate these feelings of lamentation and we have to learn to transcend them by utilizing our intelligence to fix our conscious on higher principles of life, the higher goals of life.

Krishna tells Arjuna in Gita

śrī-bhagavān uvāca
aśocyān anvaśocas tvaṁ
prajṣā-vādāṁś ca bhāṣase
gatāsūn agatāsūṁś ca
nānuśocanti paṇḍitāḥ

[Bg. 2.11]

The wise man does not lament for the living or the dead, so this lamentation is due to previous material conditioning. So we have to purify ourselves from these material conditioning through the process of purification while those anarthas, those unwanted desires are still in our heart we have to simply tolerate them. Just like if you're sick the doctor may give you some treatment which you don't like, but it's good for you, so you put your intelligence it's good for you so you do it and at the same time you tolerate the inconvenience for a higher goal, you're not willing to tolerate it for a higher goal you're not worthy of that higher go, there's always inconvenience to do something to attain something great there will always be inconvenience, we have to tolerate whatever inconvenience and push forward for that goal.

Scientist the wonderful discoveries they have made and the inventions, they are not devotees, they have spent their time in these inventions. So we take advantages of these discoveries and inventions and whatever for example a car is used to take us from one place to other place, if we just stop the progress and go on chanting the name of Lord we could never discover these things, what I am saying is …(five . forty-six)

Krishna's says in Bhagavad-Gita that he is the intelligence of the intelligence, he is the ability in man. Do you think man created the car? It was only by the intelligence given by God that man has any capacity to create anything. God is a supreme creator. So ultimately the credit goes to Krishna you don't even have a brain to think or hands to move without the grace of God that is true.

So second about because you are a devotee it doesn't mean that you're not productive in contributing to society you're the most productive and contributing to society, but you have the proper wisdom of what society needs so that you'll produce the right things with the right motives. So, let's take automobile for instance if in a God conscious society there is truly a need and a benefit for automobiles, devotees, will God not give the intelligence to a devotees? you can give the intelligence to a materialist, He can certainly give the intelligence to his devotee, but he'll use it for the right motive. Now the question is debatable whether the invention of the car was good or bad? we see the car itself if it's used in Krishna's circle since it's already made, it's already there, it's used in Krishna's service it's good, if it's used for the purpose of sinful activities or materialistic activities it's bad, from the spiritual point of view. So, it's already there, so therefore that we have nothing against anything that's already here as long as it's used for the proper motivation now we can trace back was it a good idea initially to make the car? That's debatable has it created more problems? or done more benefits? it used to be here in India that everyone would work within a few minutes’ walk from where they lived and everything was literally this is what India was. Everything was provided right in your own village, it was a simple life, a quiet life absolutely pure water, absolutely pure air, very pure consciousness, greediness and thievery was not there, because everybody basically had what they needed there was plenty of food and everybody and anything you wanted there was somebody in your little area that could provide it. There was the doctors, you didn't employ lawyers in those days, so it was a simple little it was a simple life very peaceful where people could naturally love each other, they could, the families were close together and there was a very high spiritual purpose. In the mornings the whole village would come together. This is what India was, and have kirtan and in evenings the whole village would come together and discuss Ramayan, Mahabharat. Take rest in a simple nice little house and and be happy. Now has the invention of the automobiles is good or bad? It was an artificial, it was a creation of an artificial necessity. Now in Bombay the pollution is you're smoking like 1 to 2 packs of cigarettes it's just breathing in this city, which means it's killing you were just breathing in this city is killing your body plus traffic jams just to get to work or anywhere else or to visit a friend everything's so spread out you have to be, you know through hours of traffic and people honking horns, people crashing into each other, people getting angry at each other and annoyed and swearing at each other , plus there's automobile accidents people are getting killed. So while it's already here and the society is is created in this way, hope to make the best use of a bad bargain, we'll take whatever is here and use it for Krishna's service but the question is ideally is this really the best thing for for happiness, peace, prosperity in in human society? To have all these highly technological achievements? or are they simply creating pollution, greed, disturbance and more inconvenience than convenience.

[Devotee:] [0:10:14 -0:10:27 inaudible]

[Maharaj:] Well that may be. That is true, that under the conditions of the artificial situation in society today, in order to combat the reactions we have to make artificial cures but if it was a natural society, a natural peaceful society that would probably be much less of these sorts of unnatural diseases by which you have to create all these antibiotics and so forth right?

I mean most of the diseases we're talking about our due to pollution and unnatural living.

[Devotee:] So all the progress we are making is against the spiritual life. Material life whatever [0:11:12 -0:11:18 inaudible]

[Maharaj:] All you have to do is look at the progression of human society over the years is it coming closer to God or going farther from God? Are people becoming more religious? or more materially minded? So that's a sign of the direction society is going to and other material achievements. Making big big plans for happiness through material arrangements and in the course they are forgetting the real needs of the soul and the natural devotion of God that is inherent within them.

[Devotee:] Few days back I had one patient she was two months pregnant and she was having a disease which can have congenital anomalies. The physician was all prepared to terminate the pregnancy. There were chances of the child taking birth with anomalies like short limbs any possibilities like some brain defects. So he was all prepared and what I mean is what is my role there?

[Maharaj:] Only time it's justified to kill as if it is if you're defending your own life. You don't have to kill in all circumstances, but it is justified it is not sinful to kill if it's defense. So this is the scriptural injunction so therefore if the woman, if her life is seriously and honestly being threatened by this child and she has a right to kill it to defend herself. But for any other purpose its murder, first-degree, in the eyes of God. If it's a question of how the child comes out you have no right anymore than somebody who's retarded on the streets you have no right to kill it right? If somebody's deformed on the street do you have the right to kill it just because it's deformed? That child has the right to live weather it's inside a womb or outside of the womb.

[Devotee:] So what’s [0:13:54 -0:14:01 inaudible]

[Maharaj:] By the laws of God it is Sin.

[Devotee:] Otherwise if mother continues the pregnancy her life is in danger If she continues the pregnancy, so she has right to abort?

[Maharaj:] If it's seriously and honestly analyzed, and it's not just very whimsical decided that my life is in danger so let me kill this child, out of convenience,

[Maharaj:] people have hard condition like if they continue the pregnancy, she may going to fail it.

[Maharaj:] if the doctor, he has to be honest and he is sitting before God when he makes this decision, is it an emergency life-or-death situation for the woman? If it's not then he may be implicating himself in a very sinful act.

[Devotee:] Maharaj my was asking quoting the verse where Krishna says if you remember me at the time of death surely you will come to me. My father was asking if we take gun in the streets and kill everybody, and you shoot yourself and say Hare Krishna probably you will go back to Godhead.

[Maharaj:] You will not be able to think of Krishna. Thinking of Krishna is not based on your concoction. If you are Krishna conscious and pure in your life Krishna will reveal himself as the last thought at the time of death. The fact is a person were to do that he would not be able to think of Krishna at the time of death, Krishna would not permit it.

[Devotee:] Jesus said God cannot be mocked. My father says he was saying that if you do this and if you say Hare Krishna Hare Krishna and shout yourself so that time you are thinking you of Krishna you will go back to Krishna.

[Maharaj:] You cannot think of Krishna, you can only think of Krishna only when he reveals himself, you can say Hare Krishna but Krishna will not appear in his name nor will he appear as a thought if you're doing it flagrantly. The only way you can think of Krishna at the time of death is if you prepare your life in devotion then he will reveal himself even if you're in an unconscious coma. If you have dedicated your life in the service of God he will appear before you at the time of death as the last thought at the time of death you have no control what you're gone to think. You may say Hare Krishna and then you may pull the trigger but between that Hare Krishna and you and pulling that trigger there's an interval of what you think of at the time of death. Krishna is not going to reveal himself to you to such a criminal minded person. He is going to reveal himself to someone who has practiced with great integrity during his life to be his devotee. The time of death is such a traumatic experience you cannot just choose at that time what you want to think of. It's based on what you've done in your life that Krishna reveals what you're most attached to in life is what you think of it at time of death. If you're attached to atrocities throughout your life at the time of death you're going to have an atrocious thought. if you're attached to a woman more than anything else at the time of your life at the time of death at the time of death you're going to think of that woman. If you're attached, if you cultivate attachment to Krishna more than anything else during your life then at the time of death Krishna is going to reveal himself to you as your last thought. That last thought is not based on a deliberate circumstantial decision at the instance, that thought is determined by the overall consciousness that you have cultivated throughout your life. That appears by the grace of God at the time of death. Another words that last thought is a flash of your life, the flash of your overall life's consciousness just appears as a flash to you at the time of death and that's what you think of. It's just like a final exam. do you think you could just do all nonsense and at the time of the final exam you are going to get the best grades? You're not going be able to remember. Unless you study hard to prepare for it right?

Death is the final exam according to you how you have prepared during your life you're going be prepared to think of Krishna at death. It's not at the last minute you're just going be able to think well I'll do all nonsense and all I have to do is just get good grades at the final exam. But can you get good grades unless you prepare throughout the whole semester? Impossible in college you may be able to cheat but when it comes to the time of death there's no there's nobody there's nobody's paper to look over at, you cannot cheat, it cannot cheat God. So now is the time to start preparing. Don't think you can cheat Krishna at the time of death.

[Devotee:] Why this human suffering are very selected cases in the world? Like in some cases they have miseries due to IFO, repeatedly they have this, like floods or disease in pandemic areas, this people have some disease, [0:19:53 -0:19:54 inaudible] why so is like so that?

Or other way like Americas they have, they are good looking, they are strong, they are rich, Why is it that they are rich? and Indians we are like this?

[Maharaj:] (laughter)

[Devotee:] We admire them, we look at them and strength wise they are very strong, now the records they were Indians why Indians have to be so weak?

[Maharaj:] That question could be answered in many ways, one is the records that they set, the animals could do better do you think the fastest runner in America can run as fast as a cheetah or as a tiger or or even a dog? Do you think the most powerful weightlifter in America could lift as much as an elephant? What are the records? Do you think the best gymnastic in America could do what a monkey can do? (laughter) No not possible they don't even compete.

[Devotee:] [0:21:10 -0:21:10 inaudible] Indian also cannot do that

[Maharaj:] No so what you're talking about are things that the animals could do. Human life is not meant to do what the animals could do better. When it comes to sex life a pigeon can have more sex than you can ever imagine having in one day. When it comes to gymnastics of you know wheeling around a monkey could do more with his tail than you can do it every limb of your body right?

When it comes to playing football if you put a gorilla in the field I mean everybody's going run away in total fear right ? Wrestlers, boxers you think they could fight against a gorilla and for a tiger or even a little Cobra? Put Mike Tyson in the ring with a cobra or with a scorpion or over the tiger or a lion, practically there's hundreds of species of life that can utterly destroy him within seconds in the ring right?

So is this human civilization? The animals are better at these activities of defending and athletics. So that's not advancement of human society. Advancement of human society is based on the principle of realizing your spiritual nature.

As far as prosperity, the reason why India's suffering is because India has been given the most but it's doing the least. For one who much is given much is expected. Just like you have a child how old is he? Three years old? So if he does naughty things did you take him very seriously? You give him a little punishment, but when he's 16 and he does the same things? What do you do? He punished him very severely, right? Because he knows better. Just like this child may slap his mother on the face when he's mad right child might be mad at the mother endoscope so what does the mother do? She just gives him a little slap in the face and says this is nonsense don't do this. Now what happens if the 16 year old child punches the mother in the face. It's taken more seriously he's going be punished more severely because he knows better.

So similarly spiritually the people of the West know practically nothing, they have a very very limited understanding of the purpose of life and spirituality. The Scriptures give a very very modified conception of God in the goal of life. Whereas India for millions and millions of years India has its heritage have had the greatest sages, the greatest sadhus, the hope the highest scriptures, the most complete information of God consciousness, a history of such great saints India has the greatest responsibility in the world to follow the laws of God. When India starts chasing after the things of the West the materialistic ways and leaving behind its own heritage engages in sinful activities India is going to suffer hundreds of times worse than if somebody from any other place in the world does same thing because there's so much by your birth in India you are responsible, to live according to the according to the heritage of the spiritual Nation and if you don't? You're going to be smashed by the laws of nature more than anybody else that is why there's so much poverty, that is why there is so much disease. For one who much is given much is expected.

[Devotee:] [25:20] But why they are in prosperity? They are not spiritually ….

[Maharaj:] This prosperity is such an illusion. How long do you think it's going to last? one 200 years, it is only 200 years old America. We talk India's civilization is goes back to millions and millions of years. America's only 200 years old. The persons who came to America where religious people, the founding fathers of a they left Europe for religious freedom. They were by their by their standard of religion which is of course much lower than the Indian standard, the Vedic standard, they were very pious religious people and by their piety they raped good karma so they're living the their attaining now the fruits of their good karma, their piety, their religiosity. But because now they're engaging in such sinful activities we find the whole entire system of America is falling to pieces. I mean anyone who has an open mind, who lives there sees that the whole country is coming apart by its sins. It sound the downfall it cannot survive much longer.

There's hate, there's riots, the economy is so shaky. There a huge national debt the government is taxing taxing taxing more and more the people are becoming frustrated, the pollution is becoming horrible, diseases are just plaguing the society. You'll see within your lifetime perhaps America is going fall in part and if not very soon after.

Historically every great empire that's built up when they were simple, persecuted people, striving, pious, religious they build up then they become very comfortable to become very prosperous and they become very lazy, very sinful, very decadent they don't need God anymore and then the decline. Homosexuality starts becoming prominent, lack of trust in the leadership becomes prominent.

If you read historically the fall of the Roman Empire, the fall of the Greek Empire it systematically all takes place step by step the same way. America is following all those steps exactly it's in its downfall, cannot survive. So it's just a temporary result to their pious lives. But soon they're going to be getting the temporary results for all the sins they're performing now which is going be a tremendous smashing, refreshing but not as much as India. Because India knows better the Americas hardly know better.

[Devotee:] What is your idea about the sex life between married partners?

[Maharaj:] The idea is for the purpose of service to God. In Bhagavad Gita Krishna says sex according to religious principles I am that sex. Service to God would mean that a man and a woman come together for the purpose of procreating children in this world to raise in spiritual consciousness. That is the ideal to have sex for that purpose. That sex life is yoga. That sex life is a spiritual activit,. if it's done with that motivation, for that purpose.

And of course there are certain samskaras or purificatory preparations that one undergoes before having sex life for that purpose. The Garbhadan samskara, which means according to the consciousness of the male and female at the time of sex life that will determine the type of spirit soul that enters into the womb. So therefore the male and female want to invite a very pious and religious person into this world to help the world. Varna-sankara means unwanted population. When sex, when children are conceived simply out of lust, a very low-class, very sinful nature person will take shelter of the womb in most cases in those circumstances. But when they, like for instance many parents they chant fifty malas of the Hare Krishna mantra the man & the woman perform many many other purificatory activities before having sex. In this way they are so Krishna conscious and so pure in their consciousness that they're going to attract a very very elevated soul to take shelter in their womb. And then they're going to raise that child in such a way with the responsibility to God that the child becomes peaceful happy and Krishna consciousness in its life.

[Devotee:] Desire they won’t just vanish.

[Maharaj:] But if you are engaging in positive spiritual activities you are engaging in positive spiritual activities then these desires are replaced with higher more virtuous desires. Spiritual life means to purify our desires, by spiritual activities and spiritual thoughts. It is true you cannot just give up desires, you cannot just give up your sinful desires but you can purify them by chanting the names of God, by reading the scriptures, by engaging in devotional activities, this purifies our consciousness and these lower desires can no longer remain.

Can darkness remain in the presence of the Sun? Sinful desires are due to ignorance, but Krishna is light. In the presence of the light of Krishna the sinful desires which are born of ignorance cannot remain within our heart so the main thing we have to concentrate on is filling our mind filling our consciousness filling our senses with the light of Krishna and then it will dispel all the darkness of these sinful desires that are lurking within our mind. So this is the ideal in marriage life according to the principles of Bhagavad-Gita. Now one may not be able to follow this ideal on the highest level immediately, but one should be striving in that direction and gradually through purification of the heart, through devotional activities one will be able to come to that standard.

Prahlad Maharaja in Srimad Bhagavatam describes that sex life for a materialist is nothing more than scratching an itch. It has no more significance than that you're just itching like crazy and it's not enjoyment it's just it's actually relief of anxiety and that relief of anxiety is considered by a foolish person to be pleasure right?

It's just like if you have poison ivy or poison oak some horrible rash and it's itching like crazy and it's painful it's suffering and when you scratch it it's like ah oh it's so nice right? But then what happens when you stop scratching it, it is just more than ever because you've irritated it. What do you tell a patient who has an itching rash? Do you tell them scratch it, it feels good you tell them tolerate it and it'll go away the more you scratch it the more its irritated well that's what sex life is all about. It's an itch and it itches so bad that you just have to scratch it and while you're scratching and it's just like ah so nice. But then after you scratch it what you've done is you have just irritated that itch so bad that next time it comes around it's going it's going Scratchy it itch even worse but if you tolerate the itch and take the proper medication which is the holy name of God then gradually the itch will go away.

[Devotee:] In Srimad Bhagavatam that king is having hundreds of children what is that?

[Maharaj:] If they are God-conscious they are not simply scratching itches they are propagating the world with God conscious children there's a need for God conscious children. The duty of Grihastha is to have God conscious children that means you perform the proper samskaras you have the child in the right consciousness, in pure consciousness and you invite very very elevated souls and then you raise them in God consciousness. You can have hundreds of children if you're if you're in that consciousness and you're able to maintain and protect them all spiritually. Bhaktisiddhanta Sarasvati Thakura’s Guru, our Guru Maharaj’s Guru, Param Guru he used to say that if I could make if I could raise children in this world and make them Krishna conscious, I would have hundreds of children. But he decided to take some Sannyasa, because he thought it was very difficult to do it.

But this is the duty of Grihastha however many children you can maintain and protect very very nicely in such a way that they can become Krishna conscious you have that many children. If you have too many, you will not be able to raise them properly. But it's the duty of the Grihastha to help human society by bringing in good population. It's a service to God and it's a service to humanity.

[Devotee:] [0:36:02 -0:36:05 inaudible] this question together mentally never have any idea about birth.

[Maharaj:] But you have to understand that that child has taken birth in your womb according to your karma. It's the child's fault that he has that karma of taking that body and it's your fault by your karma do you have it as a son right? Or a doctor now you have no right to kill it. Any more than outside of the womb what if you have a child, somebody has a child in the child gets in some accident and is lame blind deaf and dumb for life? Do you have a right to kill it? You may not like it as a child anymore might be completely inconvenient and embarrassing for you but do you have a right to kill it? No more outside of the womb than inside of the womb.

This whole concept of the pro-choice is so selfish all the choices for the mother but they give no choice to the child. The child is in the most innocent condition imaginable, and nobody gives it. Does anyone ask the child can we abort you? How many children you think would raise their hand and say yes kill me. Absolutely none, so yes a woman has the freedom to make choice but not when it's at the cost of the life of another human being. And a doctor is in the same position and at the end the doctor, the woman everyone involved is hundred percentage responsible for the actions they take. Human life is a life of responsibility. Killing a child in the womb according to karma, is , has no, it is none different than killing a child outside of the womb.

Whether it's living inside or living outside, it's living, it's growing, it has as much right to live inside as outside. It's like if you kill someone when they're sleeping. Is it less of a offense than to kill someone when they are awake? You may say oh well he was not conscious when I killed it. Right? Doesn't matter. Its living being.

[Devotee:] sometimes it’s a situation that either the child is in danger or mother is in danger, so? [0:38:44:3-0:38:47:1 inaudible] it’s very rare situation maharaj [0:38:49:1-0:38:49:9 inaudible].

[Maharaj:] We already discussed this and the answer is according to scripture it's sinful to kill any living being unless your life is being threatened to defend your own life you have a right to kill. You don't have to kill but you do have that right. If that child taking (womb mis speak) birth in your womb is threatening the mother's life she has a right to kill it. If it's not? And for any other motive it's first-degree murder, in the eyes of God. Now anybody can just say well it's its endanger my life because after all I have high blood pressure so (laughter). You have to face, you have to sit before God in your own mind and you have to know is this an emergency life-or-death predicament? If it is not you have no right to take its life only if it's a serious life-and-death situation, you have any right in the eyes of God of taking the life of that child. It cannot be a maybe this or maybe that situation. It has to be definite specific life-or-death.

The doctor's karmically has the same responsibility as the mother. If the doctor is implicated by his decision or by his operation in killing a child specifically and exclusively for the emergency protection of the woman's life, the doctor has no Karma. But if it's for any other purpose? And if God doesn't see it the same way you do (laughter) that the doctor has pulled the trigger. And he has performed first-degree murder in the eyes of God and according to the laws of karma.

[Devotee:] Maharaj my mother performs abortions regularly weather its necessary to [0:40:59 -0:41:03 inaudible] I part take of karma? Now I have told but she doesn’t want to agree or change her ways so what should I do that.. why should I get karma on my head?

[Maharaj:] You should be very careful not to take any of her money and use it for your sense gratification.

[Devotee:] [0:41:30 -0:41:37 inaudible]

[Maharaj:] You better offer all your food to Krishna, you should try to convince her. Your love for her will be to convince her

[Devotee:] [0:41:45 -0:42:19 inaudible] She doesn’t, I tried she doesn’t want to get convince I tired. So I am really of my patience I wonder what should I do? … just accept that its special arrangement that I have to partake of her karma, …. in that situation …. So I have to, as long as she performs her

[Maharaj:] The integrity of your life before God is you should support yourself with honest you should not maintain your body with money that is a product of sin.

[Devotee:] [0:42:35 -0:42:42 inaudible] Then maharaj what should I do?

[Maharaj:] As long as you're in the situation you are now you should understand it's very unfortunate and you should become very very serious about your spiritual life and pray to God to forgive you for whatever unavoidable circumstances you're in. But as soon as possible you should try to create a separate provision for yourself.

[Devotee:] [0:43:04 -0:43:16 inaudible]

[Maharaj:] If his whatever you do for Krishna its by honest means it's right. But you're taking money from the government. Where are you taking?

[Devotee:] [0:43:40 -0:44:14 much part of part is transcribed but few words are inaudible] Maharaj outside the section I want to ask you. suppose, a person is not studied hard if he had studied hard he can compete with open .. people with high class people not middle class people. He can study hard, if he is a devotee, he has realized himself there is no caste, he is a spirit soul. You should study hard and compete with ,… open .. and then desire a post and then …. What is your comment on that? Why should he take benefit of reserved cast? If he is a devotee, he has realized that he is a spirit soul, supposed to be spirit soul, servant of Krishna, no cast noting, so why doesn’t he work hard and take a ..

[Maharaj:] The question the question is what is he doing with his free time? In other words if he's engaging in positive spiritual activities, in his reserved time and he's taking the minimum that he has to be required to get his education that is also perfect.

[Devotee:] He is not taking money.

[Maharaj:] Time, what is he doing with his time? Sinful & material is how you're utilizing your time? For instance if that person is is is let's say in the medical field, he's doing medical camps and he's helping thousands of people become healthier and better and Krishna con… [44:55 Recording break]

[Devotee:] [0:44:55 -0:45:30 much part of part is transcribed but few words are inaudible] Maharaj outside the section I want to ask you. suppose, a person have not studied hard if he had studied hard he can compete with open ... people, with high class people not middle class people. He can study hard, if he is a devotee, he has realized himself there is no caste, he is a spirit soul, you should study hard and compete with ,… open .. and then desire a post and then …. What is your comment on that? Why should he take benefit of reserved cast? If he is a devotee, he has realized that he is a spirit soul, supposed to be spirit soul, servant of Krishna, no cast noting, so why doesn’t he work hard and take a ..

[Maharaj:] The question the question is what is he doing with his free time? In other words if he's engaging in positive spiritual activities, in his reserved time and he's taking the minimum that he has to be required to get his education that is also perfect.

[Devotee:] He is not taking money.

[Maharaj:] Time, what is he doing with his time? Sinful & material is how you're utilizing your time? For instance if that person is is is let's say in the medical field, he's doing medical camps and he's helping thousands of people become healthier and better and Krishna consciousness and because of that service he just as the absolute bare minimum to get his degrees, that's perfect because he's utilizing his time for Krishna consciousness. Now if that person is going to cinemas and nightclubs and in order to enjoy those things he does the absolute minimum to get his degree and then that's sinful. If the utilization of the time is for the right purpose then his overall plan is according to the will of God. [Devotee:] The basic question is, he could have studied hard he could have completed with open [0:47:10- 0:47:11 inaudible]

[Maharaj:] That is who, that is nothing to do with God, competing with the higher classes if you have to compete with the higher classes and do it but if you can utilize your energies more in the service of God and obtain the same result that's better ah! The question is how you utilizing your time? That's how God determines whether it's good or bad.

[Devotee:] [0:47:45- 0:48:23 much of question is inaudible however transcribed few words] the thing is that this candidate X who studied extremely well, he is also quite …. Not in a way … as the the person and Y is the other person who was not studying at all but he is chanting names of Krishna and all, and he is going around here and there but he is a reserved candidate, supposed to be reserved candidate ……. Why should he … a seat and if … the seat is he not sinning?

[Maharaj:] No he is he is sinning if he's using his life his time sinfully, he's using his life for God's service we can take any facility or anything

[Devotee:] [0:48:23- 0:48:38 inaudible]

[Maharaj:] The question is what is a utilizing it's time for? is if is if it's for the service of God it's Krishna conscious, if it's for the service of a senses its sinful no matter, even if that man works like an ass and earns the competition that he deserves if he's not doing it for God he's a sinful fool and someone who just does gets by with the least amount but he's doing it with the proper motivation for God he's perfect. And if the man works very hard and is in the fierce competition and comes out number one but he does it for God he's he's perfect. The question is what are your motives? And what are you doing it for?

[Devotee:] Ya so

[Maharaj:] We're not concerned with the mundane concept of whether it's fair or not fair? Because practically 99% of the people who are living fairly are not doing it for God their sinful.

[Devotee:] That person does it for God and this person was also told to do it for God but he is not getting it because this fellow is going to get it what is the..

[Maharaj:] That's not the case, let's be practical how many people are doing it for God in this society?

[Devotee:] Maharaj another thing is

[Maharaj:] ah! Answer my question. How many people? This is such a bizarrely theoretically impractical question nobody's doing it for God except a few. So if one person is doing it, the odds are the person who's going get it is not a saintly person dedicating his life in the service of the Lord such a person is rare, one out of thousands, one out of millions. However he sees fit he can serve God whether it's taking the if the the the convenience of the facilities are working hard either way as long as the motives and the purpose is correct, you are perfect.

[Devotee:] Any who has become vaishnava he related himself to the highest-level right? Why does he have to attain I mean comes down to lower level to take admission and so far

[Maharaj:] He doesn't have to but if it but if it's if it if he can serve God and serve humanity better by doing that he should. Don't you think? If a man is dying and one person just in an emergency situation just completely unorthodox saves his life and another person well I have to go through all the proper official legal standards of treating this person and the person dies who's better? The self righteous person is going a going call the legalistic rules or the person who saves the man's life no matter what it takes? Right? We're not so concerned with all these official college policies and these ideas of what's fair and what's not fair the whole world is practically unfair. Fairness just like we were discussing the other day telling the truth in a life of lies that truth is just part of the lie right? If you're living a lie truth within that life is part of the lie, is it not? At the same time if you're living according to the truth and according to the standard in order to live according to the truth you have to lie that lie is part of the truth.

Just like one girl was asking, my parents absolutely will not permit me to chant Hare Krishna or to meet the devotees, so the question is if the only way I can live a Krishna conscious life is to lie to my parents and tell them I'm going to visit somebody and go to the temple is that a lie? I told her if you're not Krishna conscious your whole life is a lie so if you're telling your parents the truth it's just truth within a lie that truth is part of a big lie, the fact is you are the you are you are part and parcel of Krishna and your constitutional position is to serve Krishna and everything outside of that is an illusion it's a lie so telling the truth to just further perpetuate a lie is that the truth? No. But if you are if she's coming to live a life in devotion and she has to tell a lie in order to do a life live live a life of devotion she's telling a lie in order to live for truth. Whereas the other person is telling the truth in order to live for a lie. So which is better?

So similarly the if everybody is studying and competing how many people are doing it for God? People are doing it for money and prestige, ninety nine percent of the population of these students are doing it for money and prestige. So if one person is really dedicating his life in the service of God whatever, whichever way he can best serve God it's truth. Even if it may appear dishonest by completely mundane standards he's reaching out and helping people by doing it that's a higher truth than just doing everything very honorably and doing nothing for anyone except your own senses. Ah! so he doesn't have to do that but if that's the best way he can serve the Lord if that's the way he could use his energies best for the upliftment of humanity he should do it that way. Is that clear?

[Devotee:] [0:54:18- 0:54:34 inaudible] if devotee sanyasi walking by the road and one is drawing in the water, in the river is it the same? .. say?

[Maharaj:] That principle, the the story is they were two sadhus walking and they came to a river and there was a young lady who was unable to cross the river so very helplessly she asked can you help me cross the river? So once sadhu picked her up and they crossed the river and then he put her down and then they walked about five kilometres and the other sadhu who he was in so much anxiety, finally he could not control his mind any longer and he just began to chastise his friend how is it possible that you carried a young woman across the river? How is this possible? This is not right I can't tolerate it and the sadhu the first sadhu looked at him he said the difference between you and me is I I put her down five kilometers away but you've been carrying her in your mind up until this point.

So we can be very self-righteous but if our motivation is incorrect our self-righteousness has no spiritual value. Ah! Better it's better to be to deviate slightly from the official order of the ritual or the proper way if your motivation is correct than to follow everything perfectly that is what Jesus Christ condemned in the Hebrew priests of his time. They were performing all the rituals exactly according to the scripture but they were doing it for name fame and prestige and money. Ah! whereas Jesus in order to reach out and help people he was breaking one rule after another. He was in what he was in the house of the Pharisees one day, the Pharisees were the priests and this woman came she was a prostitute, who was not even allowed to step into the house but she was completely repentant and wanted to just surrender at the feet of Jesus and take shelter of God. So he told her she could come in now they were completely horrified how can you let a low-class untouchable women like this in our house? And then she began to cry and she began to bathe Jesus's feet with her tears and she dried it with her hair and the Pharisees utterly disgusted by Jesus they just were criticizing him all over Jerusalem all over the town after they saw this. How can you allow this to happen? But Jesus was giving shelter and made a pure devotee out of this woman by accepting her repentance, by facilitating even though he broke some rules and doing it. Whereas the Pharisees they could not change anyone's heart, they could not bring anyone closer to God in any way although they were doing everything perfectly. So who's better? Jesus are the Pharisees? You don't know the name of any of the Pharisees but everyone knows Jesus that's the difference. It's a question of our spirit, our heart not how not how strictly we're following the rules of the social conduct in the moral conduct.

Ah! If you're actually doing it for the betterment of humanity in the service of God with devotion that is perfect whichever way you choose to do it as long as that's truly your motive it's not such a sinful thing whereas ninety nine percent of these students who are as you say they're earning the right to be in the, in that particular class of studies what are they doing it for? Who are they doing it for? They might be doing everything just according to the way it should be done but it's like so many zeros, it adds up to nothing because they're not doing it for the supre… according to the goal of life, for the purpose of human life they're simply doing it for their own aggrandizement. They're like the Pharisees externally, superficially they're first-class perfect but their hearts are not in the right place. It's better to act like a third-class man if your hearts in the right place right? If that's what's necessary in your service. Ah! of course, if it's not absolutely necessary but it's best to act like a first-class man with the right with your heart in the right place. But as a doctor isn't it sometimes to save a person you have to put aside all the formalities of what should be done it shouldn't be done because it's an emergency? If a doctors not willing to do that because he's so attached to the proper method of doing things that means he has no compassion he's not concerned with the patient, he's simply concerned with his own official process of in self-righteousness and how things must be done right? A good doctor is a doctor he'll do anything and everything to save his patient even if it means breaking all the rules, isn't that true? (laughter)

Jesus was put into court too. Haridas Thakur was also put into court too and they were both found guilty right? Haridas Thakur was taken before the judge, and they they, it was a quick trial but they charged him, they found him guilty and they told him he was to be beaten in twenty two market places until he was dead. Jesus Christ they took him before the judge who happened to be Pontius Pilot he was the judge, the person who gave the ultimate decisions in criminal matters at the time and he ordered him to be killed, to be crucified. Since time immemorial, what to speak of Prahlada Maharaja? Prahlada maharaja's father sentenced him to death on so many occasions but of course he could not kill him. So it's the nature of the demonic class they're always trying to destroy those who are pure and sacred within society, because there's such a threat. Simply religious persecution that's all. It's just the modern-day way of persecuting people.

[Devotee:] [1:01:30- 1:01:38 inaudible]

[Maharaj:] Because it's the devotee as far as possible to defend what belongs to God. Why didn't Arjuna just say to Krishna I'm not going to fight, why should I retaliate? Why shouldn't I just leave it up to you and give it to the Kurus? Ah! because it was Arjuna's duty as Krishna's to devotee to pretend to protect the truth and protect what belong to God, the whole earth belongs to God. Arjuna was meant to fight for the truth on behalf of Krishna. Fight so that what belonged to Krishna would be used for Krishna. Ah! your question challenges the very spirit of Bhagavad Gita. Krishna tells Arjuna and tells all of us through Arjuna to stand up and fight for Krishna. Ah! whether it's in the courtrooms are in the battlefield or on the streets or within your own mind. (Laughter) We all have to fight for Krishna. Ah! how many of us, our minds are our best friend? We have so many enemies in our own mind, practically our whole mind is our worst enemy, [1:03:08- 1:03:10 inaudible]? We have to fight for Krishna.

[Devotee:] Also mushrooms, why we should not eat mushrooms?

[Maharaj:] You can explain the answer.

[Devotee:] [1:03:35- 1:03:58 inaudible] Maharaj, in the battlefield of Kurukshetra why did Arujna killed King Vena, Karna ..

[Maharaj:] How can you question God? First of all you have to ask is Krishna of God? If if you accept Krishna is God you have to accept He is all good and therefore whatever he does is for the ultimate good of everyone involved. If he's not God then you can question him according to these rational logical mundane standards of morality. Right? The real question is Krishna the absolute truth? If he is? Then whatever he does is perfect. If he isn't then he's subject to the same laws we are. Now according to the Mahabharata for this book is coming the book of Mahabharat according to that Scripture he is the absolute truth he is the supreme Brahman he happened to show the universal form to Arjuna before he told him to kill Karna. So Krishna's decisions are perfect because he is perfect everything he does is perfect.

Karna got liberated, Karna went back to Godhead. Think that was an injustice? If he wasn't killed for all the criminal activities he committed and all the offenses he would have gone to hell but because he was killed in the presence of God he went back to Godhead, he got the supreme liberation immediately. [1:05:25- 1:05:26 inaudible] think he objected to that? May be while it was taking place he did but as soon as it was over with he was very happy [Laughter] Right?

Krishna wanted the Pandavas to win for the sake of all humanity if the Kurus would have won that war it would have been a disastrous condition for all humanity because they were leading the people in irreligion. He wanted the people led toward religion, now Karna was the only hope of the Kurus to defeat the Pandavas because he had the power to kill Arjuna and without Arjuna the Pandavas could not have won that war. So therefore Krishna saw that the benefit of all humanity was more important than one than one little Kshatriya code being broken, right?

[Devotee:] But then Krishna was in, Krishna is Supreme Personality of Godhead, since he was with Arujna could not Arjuna Killed Karna by fair means also

[Maharaj:] Yes Krishna wanted to show that a devotee is even too willing to surrender, his good name and fame of being a good moralist if it's if it's to serve the will of God. Sometimes we're very proud of being very moral and that that pride is also a spiritual obstacle a devotee should be willing to surrender anything for the service of God, right?

[Devotee 1:] Maharaj Bhima, Bhima killed in wrong way [1:06:56- 1:06:59 inaudible]

[Devotee 2:] Maharaj television, television series is going on , Mahabharat, Person who is playing Krishna, he is very attractive person, so whenever I or many people whenever we remember, try to remember Krishna I always get image of that person, named Krishna, is it wrong?

[Maharaj:] Better if you come to Radha-Gopinath temple more often and fix your mind (Laughter) It's better to fix your mind on him then and some movie actress displaying Maya (Laughter)

[Devotee:] [1:07:46- 1:07:47 inaudible]

[Maharaj:] Ah so if he's reminding you of Krishna that's good, but as I say it's best if you come to the temple and fix your minor the real Krishna.

[Devotee:] [1:08:07- 1:08:15 inaudible] What ..

[Maharaj:] It depends on your Karma. Your subtle body could carry your mind intelligence and ego to your next body but how is how it is carried? Is according to your activities and desires in this life. There are instances in history someone like Mozart when he was 5 years old he was he was writing beautiful symphonies nobody even taught him how to do it? How is that possible? Because by his karma he carried on the intelligence that he had in his previous life their stories in the Srimad Bhagavatam of Jad Bharat Maharaj where he was the continuity of his same think thinking processes carried on to three births. Ah! So that's very unusual Karma. But according to your karma you can you can carry on the assets that you achieve in this world or if you misuse your intelligence in this even if you have very very good intelligence but you misuse it in this world if you lose it all it can be all taken away.

[Devotee:] Live a extremely pious life now will be free from cycle of life and death?

[Maharaj:] No He lived the pious life, the most you do is you get prosperity in your next life until you surrender to God you have to continue on this cycle of birth and death. Until you awaken your love of God, until you go beyond piety and impiety, until you attain the state of pure bhakti, pure devotion you must keep taking birth. Pious pious activities will force you to take another birth. It's according to laws of karma you have to accept the results of your actions, there's no room, if you give charity to somebody you have to come back to receive that charity back from that person. You have no choice. Ah! And if you committed if you commit an offense to a person you have to come back to accept the result of that offense. You do good work you have to come back to reap the results of the good work, if you do bad work you have to come back to reap the results of the bad work but if you engage in God's service? If your life is surrendered in the service of the Lord? That is beyond the good and bad of this world, Bhakti is transcendental. If our activities are based on the principle of bhakti we never have to take birth again in this material world. Because there is no Karma in devotional service.

[Devotee:] What happens to bad karma that already I have until now? All the bad karma I have until now what happens to that? If I take devotional life properly

[Maharaj:] When you, Krishna says in Gita

sarva-dharmān parityajya
mām ekaṁ śaraṇaṁ vraja
ahaṁ tvāṁ sarva-pāpebhyo
mokṣayiṣyāmi mā śucaḥ

[Bg. 18.66]

Abandon all varieties of religion and just surrender to me I shall relieve you from all sinful reactions do not fear.

When you surrender to Krishna no matter how many sins you have committed, has it makes no difference. Krishna assumes all those sins himself and cleanses your heart and cleanses your cleanses you from all the karmic reaction for whatever you have done, the moment you truly in your heart surrender to Krishna. I must go.

[Devotee:] [1:12:40- 1:12:44 inaudible]

[Maharaj:] is there more any very important questions before?

[Devotee:] [1:12:47- 1:13:05 inaudible] finally when I go back to to Godhead then last time I was there whatever this body we have to leave here, when we go there we don’t remember anything, we are going to enjoy there in serving Krishna …… in Vridnavan anything about …. This is the end here?

[Maharaj:] You can remember anything you want to remember.

[Devotee:] Can?

[Maharaj:] He desire, yes you are all knowing, the spirit soul is full of knowledge, you can remember any of your experiences in this material world if that's what you want. But when you're with Krishna your mind is very happy, the experiences in this world are incomparable to the joy of the spiritual world so you're not going to be so anxious to remember these things (laughter). But if you can, if you want you can. It is the same soul that is here that will be there and that soul is full of knowledge. Ah! But in that mood of pure devotion, if it distracts your mind away from your service to Krishna you will not want to. But if somebody has helped you in your spiritual life in this world you'll easily remember them say to Krishna please take, help that person come to join us (laughter). That's natural, all right? If somebody you love very much in this world is still here you'll say to Krishna I love that person very much let's help him come and be happy with us ah! and Krishna will listen.

[Devotee:] [1:14:34- 1:14:36 inaudible] (laughter)

[Maharaj:] Just like often times often times often times devotees speak like this amongst each other that when you go back to Godhead please remember me and you know tell Krishna that I'm still here (laughter). It works like that also, so it's good to make friends with devotees who are going back to Godhead (laughter), you gets special connections (laughter).

[Devotee 1:] [1:15:02- 1:15:03 inaudible]

[Devotee 2:] Maharaj please remember us (laughter)

[Maharaj:] I'll try but where I'll be I don't know, if you want me to remember [Laughter].

[Devotee:] [1:15:18- 1:15:21 inaudible]

[Maharaj:] [1:15:21- 1:15:21 inaudible]

[Devotee:] [1:15:22- 1:15:30 inaudible]

[Maharaj:] So when will we see you again?

[Devotee:] Today evening.

[Maharaj:] Today evening? Ah!.

[Devotee:] [1:15:43- 1:15:47 inaudible]

© Radhanath Swami Lecture Transcripts.
